artifact.content_preview:
GC-CLOUD WAVE 4 — MONTHLY STATUS REPORT
Reporting Period: February 2026
Report Date: March 1, 2026
Project Manager: Carol Nguyen
Status: GREEN

---

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

GC-Cloud Wave 4 is progressing excellently. The team has made strong
progress across all workstreams and stakeholder engagement remains high.
We are confident in our ability to deliver the project on schedule and
within the approved parameters.

---

SCHEDULE HIGHLIGHTS

The project timeline is performing well. While we have exper

extracted_tasks.text: will be submitted to the Director for consideration.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: will be submitted to the Director for consideration.

risks.description: The project has already consumed three weeks of schedule contingency due to infrastructure assessment delays, reducing the buffer available for future unforeseen issues before the Q3 2026 deadline.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The infrastructure assessment phase required a slight adjustment of approximately three weeks... this has been fully accommodated within the project's existing schedule contingency.

risks.description: The project is currently trending 14% over the approved budget envelope due to newly discovered middleware remediation requirements, requiring formal approval for additional funds.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The estimated cost of this remediation is $340,000, representing approximately 14% above the approved envelope of $2,400,000.

risks.description: Discovery of middleware remediation requirements suggests that the original scope baseline was incomplete, posing a risk of further technical 'unknown unknowns' during the migration phase.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The infrastructure assessment identified additional middleware remediation requirements not captured in the original scope baseline.

risks.description: The project is entering a procurement phase in Q2 2026; any delays in government procurement processes or lack of qualified vendors could delay the implementation start date.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The project team looks forward to entering the implementation phase in Q2 2026 following completion of the procurement activities.

risks.description: Potential misalignment between the project's 'Green' status and the actual financial/scope health, which may lead to stakeholder friction when the 14% budget overrun is formally presented.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Status: GREEN... The estimated cost of this remediation is $340,000, representing approximately 14% above the approved envelope

risks.description: The 'refinement of the cost model' and 'updated cost projections' suggest that the final migration costs are still volatile and may exceed the current $340,000 overrun once implementation begins.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The findings from this assessment are being used to refine our cost model... We are in the process of finalizing updated cost projections
